Costing Tool
This is the first real browser slice of the full web port of Master Costing Tool - Stormwater.xlsm. It already uses workbook-extracted lookup data and cascading BOQ selectors, and we’ll keep layering the remaining workbook behaviour on top.

This uses workbook-extracted table data from the original costing XLSM. The next passes will add more workbook-specific behaviours like deeper modifier logic, panel/source handling, and any macro-driven output workflows still missing.
Relining Work Orders
No files loaded
Upload Required Files
Packaging Rules
Run Packaging
Packaging runs locally in the browser against the uploaded files.
Packaging Reasoning: Upload files and preview candidates to see the current relining rules applied to the data.
Size Groups — diameters in the same group can share packages. Earlier groups win if they overlap.
Top-up rule for underfilled packages
Cost Modifiers
Generated Packages
Generate relining packages to see package counts and cost totals.
Package Map
Relining Package Map
Generate packages to draw package groups on the map.
Edit Packages
Move pipes between generated packages. Manual moves persist across regenerations until cleared. Empty packages are kept. To create a new package, choose + Create new package in any pipe's Move to package dropdown.
Generate packages on the Pipe Relining tab first to enable manual editing.
Future Works Plan
This is the web-side staging view for the SharePoint workbook. It is shaped around the 15) Pipe Renewal Backlog sheet so we can review and edit relining package rows here first, then wire the same row shape into SharePoint and Excel.
Workbook Target
Workbook
2027-2031 CAPEX Project Brief - Program of Works Details - E&R - Rolling.xlsx
Sheet
15) Pipe Renewal Backlog
Source
Latest relining package generation
Council Program Folder
%USERPROFILE%\OneDrive - Northern Beaches Council\Stormwater Engineering - General\Program
Expected Workbook Path
%USERPROFILE%\OneDrive - Northern Beaches Council\Stormwater Engineering - General\Program\2027-2031 CAPEX Project Brief - Program of Works Details - E&R - Rolling.xlsx
Direct SharePoint workbook editing is the next step. This first pass keeps the row structure aligned with the backlog sheet so the later Graph sync can write the same fields into a machine-managed table. The local OneDrive path above is the expected Council-synced location for normal Excel editing.
SharePoint Workbook Access
This opens the full workbook in the normal SharePoint/Excel experience so you can edit every sheet directly, not just the relining backlog.

If SharePoint access is unreliable, load the forward works workbook and the relining output manually here. The page will use the uploaded files in-browser for staging and mapping.
Relining Mapping Defaults
Backlog Preview
Generate relining packages first, then click Load Latest Relining Packages here to stage them into the future works backlog format.
CCTV Work Order Packaging
Separate tool from relining packaging. This workflow filters condition 1 to 6 components, excludes assets with CCTV in the last 4 years, then builds proximity-first packages with only a light criticality influence. The engine is kept portable so it can move into ESRI / TechnologyOne CiA cleanly later.
No files loaded
Upload Required Files
Assets: not loaded  ·  Rates: not loaded
Packaging Rules
Optional post-processing step. Original small packages are moved once into the nearest original non-small package within this separate distance.
Packaging Reasoning: Run a preview to see the current rules applied to the uploaded data.

Upload files and run the preview to see remaining CCTV candidates.

Critical Assets
Inspection Map
Interactive aerial map of Northern Beaches critical stormwater assets. Pipe assets are drawn from start/end coordinates, and non-pipe assets are shown as point markers.

Source files are loaded by upload because this static site cannot read local folders. The legacy D:\Packaging prototype is stale; this page is the packaging source of truth.
Asset Class Filter
Inspection Status Filter
Map Legend
Overdue
Due within 6 months
Up to date
Awaiting schedule
Schedule Logic
The page calculates status from either a supplied next due date, or from the last inspection date plus the inspection interval. Orange is set to assets due within the next 183 days.
